description_long: This object is a classic submachine gun, visually reminiscent of the Thompson M1, featuring a robust black metal receiver and barrel assembly paired with rich, dark wooden furniture. The stock is a solid, fixed wooden piece, providing stability and a traditional aesthetic, while the foregrip and pistol grip are also crafted from wood, offering ergonomic handling. The magazine is a detachable box type, inserted vertically in front of the trigger guard, and the barrel is of standard length, extending forward with a simple cylindrical profile. Iron sights are mounted on the top, with a rear notch and a front post for aiming. The overall finish is a combination of matte black metal and polished wood, giving it a vintage yet functional appearance. No muzzle device is attached to the end of the barrel, maintaining a clean and straightforward silhouette.
description: A vintage-style submachine gun with a black metal body and polished wooden stock, grips, and forearm. It features a detachable box magazine, iron sights, and a standard barrel with no muzzle device.
description_view_0: Front view showing the barrel's tip, iron sight post, and the narrow profile of the wooden foregrip and stock.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the full length of the rifle, the fixed wooden stock, wooden foregrip, pistol grip, and the detachable box magazine.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the wooden stock and the alignment of the receiver and barrel.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the same features as view 1, with clear visibility of the magazine, trigger, and wooden components.
